item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from Wikimedia Commons under the same license.

item.title: Re: Indestructible?
item.content:encoded: I'd imagine they'd do something like that eventually, but it's not a huge problem at the moment. There are a number of scips that show the perils of cross-testing, and we don't even know what this stuff is: for all we know, it could turn the book in 826 into the Ladybug Bible, causing it to spew out an endless stream of these things. Or, it could warp 507's abilities so that it manifests only in universes full of the stuff, carrying it back every time it teleports until it dies of cancer. There's no way of telling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How could you, Fingo?

I will never touch insects again.

glad i have arachnids

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better say that the image used is the work the user here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/User:Charlesjsharp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"What if bugs but they poop FOREVER?"

>:(

How you managed to make this a viable article is something I will never a) understand and b) forgive you for. Take my upvote and leave me in peace.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this because it got me thinking: How powerful IS their God? Is it like a reality bending bug? Really cool to think about!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haldane's vindication, as SCP.

+1

item.title: Indestructible?
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, this is clearly an SCP where the Foundation has a "Maybe we really should get rid of this thing" attitude about it. All the experiments clearly point to this being something we should probably kill.

But it's indestructible.

So I just thought of an idea. Why don't get a large tank of the waste (or even the ladybugs themselves), and just attach it to SCP-507. Simply put, we can dumb the waste in another dimension. The Foundation has a tonne of "other dimensions" we can throw stuff into. If we're scared of putting the the ladybugs in another dimension, just throw the waste there. Trade it to SCP-702, get Mr Deeds to clean the waste up, write "The Super Awesome, Immortal Friendly Guy Who is Loyal to the Foundation and is Able to Digest and Remove From Existence the Excretion Made by the Entity we Call SCP-2794 and is Always Hungry for it." then use 826. It's not hard to get rid of stuff when you have this much removal options.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooh kinky.
Oh I mean uh that sounds so wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But then there's the risk they'll come out on somewhere else, maybe out in space giving them time to make a giant megastructure of poop, or in the centre of the earth where they can make it overflow?
Or maybe they don't have infinite dimensions in the universe this is written in? Not all scips have to align with a super-strict canon, don't they? \:3
Oooo spooky!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just put them in one of the infinite dimensions the foundation has access to

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah ok, will do :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry for saying another thing but even if I really love this SCP, I think you should add clarification to the fact that it was the drone talking in the interview since it's kinda confusing at first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You'd get your finger stuck in the tiny ladybug tunnels :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's awesome! Never knew, but yay, another connection! :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would happen if a human such as myself went into that dome?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so happy this is finally doing well. All hail ever-shitting ladybugs. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which is also funny because in Dutch we call these 'Lieveheersbeestjes' which roughly translates to 'The good Lords' critters'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow I really liked this it is such a great idea. Keep up the good work hope you make another skip soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:3 fixed all the stuff listed, thanks!
Also, a I don't mind a bit of fun in the discussions, but I appreciate Kal and shaggy looking out for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And now I'm back to say thanks, to:

All the guys in the forums,
HUGE thanks to site 19 (almost everyone there has read this one twice)
Also thanking site 17 for providing at the ready feedback

Also, I fixed the errors listed above, so I hope that'll improve reading for others to come :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't get rid of it because it'll go 682 on us but at least the religious stuff made up for it. Reminded me of SCP-2610.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thoughts on this article are essentially identical. Just to expand on one point: as you mentioned, there are a couple of bits here that are potentially confusing or poorly described. I think this is probably symptomatic of the author not having thought precisely and thoroughly enough about the world they're depicting, rather than just a simple failure of communication. Related to that is the rather odd mishmash of ideas, which you also alluded to.

Kind of weird to refer to the author in the third person on their own comment page, so let me address you directly: the starting idea of the magic butt ladybirds is pleasingly weird, but you take it to a slightly dissatisfying place with a disjointed and incomplete development of ideas. More detailed descriptions would help a lot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You never describe the size of the globe, so I pictured a small globe with ladybugs on it. Then people went inside of it.

I felt the beginning of the article was really interesting, but i liked it less as I read. The temple was meh to me and the interview at first was confusing. I then realized it was the drone, but you should put some sort of heading on the interview so we know who's talking. I wasn't thrilled about the interview.

We have lots of "exposition through fanatical ranting" SCPs on this site. I think I've tired of this trope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so I wanted to be one of the first things people see

I really would not appreciate something like this being the first thing people see in my discussion pages. I typically avoid the initial comment, because it's something I hope the author can use to say their piece or give their thanks. Wasting it just to ecstatically jump up and down isn't cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't see the footnote until after I read it. That cleared so much up and made me love it that much more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Needs line editing. There are typos, XXXX, and some inconsistencies vis-a-vis the genus name.

On the whole, I'm just slightly mystified by this. I like the connection suggested by the footnote but the direction this goes in… It's halfway between "What?" and "What?" No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You still have SCP-XXXX in the exploration log, so you might wanna fix that. Otherwise, great! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep. I was excited for this to go up, and was having a silly moment. As I said, I'm going to change it to something more suited for this site soon. Soon probably being right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FIRST!

Sorry, that was extremely immature

You're right, it was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw this when it was a brainstorming post in the forums, and immediately loved the idea, and since then I have only gotten more and more excited for this thing to go up. I love the direction you took it, I love the fact that they really are called "little messiahs" (I'm sure that you saw that fact before you made them be religious), and I love the fact that they have "little christ" inscribed on all of them.

You've done a great job, and I believe in this skip! A couple of questions though… How are they dealing with the waste? Also, at what rate is the waste coming out? And the last thing, if they couldn't send them up into space because they were conscious and got excited, why don't they put them to sleep like they did in another test?

Really really good skip. I like it a lot. +1 and more if I could.
